<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Orchestration scripts (cli.js, ci.js, web.js) delegate to package CLIs without importing library code — this enforces the four-tier dependency hierarchy described in CLAUDE.md
- Root-level config files (.npmrc, package.json, tsconfig) serve as the single source of truth for monorepo-wide tooling settings
- claude-integration.js classified as a service at the root level suggests a cross-cutting integration concern that may benefit from being documented or moved into a dedicated package
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling reflect correct orchestration-layer design — root scripts spawn CLIs without importing domain packages directly.
- claude-integration.js at the root level sits outside the established four-tier hierarchy; document its role or migrate it into the hench or llm-client package to keep the architecture explicit.
- 27 files in the root zone is relatively large; periodic review can prevent config drift and catch files that belong in a package.
- Forms the apex of the four-tier hierarchy with zero incoming edges from any other zone — confirming it is a pure consumer that nothing depends on, which is structurally correct for an orchestration layer
- project-orchestration-root has zero incoming imports from all other zones, confirming its position as the dependency-graph sink — this is the correct topology for an orchestration tier and should be enforced by lint rules to prevent accidental inversion.
- claude-integration.js is unclassified in the four-tier hierarchy — it is neither a domain package nor a pure orchestration script, making it an implicit fifth tier that violates the documented architecture
- claude-integration.js sits at the root level outside the four-tier dependency hierarchy defined in CLAUDE.md. It is classified as a 'service' but is not delegated to from cli.js the way other orchestration scripts delegate to package CLIs. Its actual import surface and responsibilities are undocumented, making it an invisible coupling risk at the orchestration tier.
- claude-integration.js is the only file in the stable-core cluster without a corresponding CLI delegation pattern — every other root script (cli.js, web.js, ci.js) spawns a subprocess, but claude-integration.js acts as a library shim, making it architecturally ambiguous between orchestration and foundation tiers
- The root zone contains 27 files but zero incoming call graph edges from any other zone, yet has 418 internal calls — this means all intra-zone coupling is between config/tooling files that call each other, not between orchestration scripts and domain code, which is the correct structural pattern
- claude-integration.js is the sole file in the orchestration tier that behaves as a library (imported) rather than a CLI runner (spawned). Document this explicitly in CLAUDE.md or migrate it to llm-client/hench. Leaving it undocumented means future contributors may model new cross-cutting concerns on it, gradually eroding the four-tier boundary.
- [call graph] 418 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
